neighborhoods.name: Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: Colonial historic center near the waterfront with Parque de los Novios, cathedral, and Gold Museum
neighborhoods.character: Colonial waterfront, cultural, compact and walkable

housing.predominantType: Renovated colonial buildings and older apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Santa Marta. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Santa Marta

neighborhoods.name: Bello Horizonte
neighborhoods.description: Residential hillside neighborhood with ocean views, shopping centers, and newer development
neighborhoods.character: Hillside residential, ocean views, growing amenities
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Santa Marta's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Santa Marta

neighborhoods.name: Mamatoco
neighborhoods.description: Eastern neighborhood near the Sierra Nevada foothills with affordable housing and local markets
neighborhoods.character: Affordable residential, gateway to mountains, local feel
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Santa Marta

neighborhoods.name: El Rodadero
neighborhoods.description: Beach resort area south of the center popular with tourists and expats, with restaurants and nightlife
neighborhoods.character: Beach resort, tourist-friendly, dining and nightlife
neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Santa Marta
